executive_summary: The Iran–US armed conflict has escalated to MAJOR (Level 1/5) with 518 incidents in the last 24 hours. The most critical development is the confirmed killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in coordinated US-Israeli strikes on Tehran (Feb 28). Iran has responded with widespread missile and drone attacks across 14 US bases in the Middle East, ballistic missile barrages on Israel, and strikes on Gulf states (UAE, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, Qatar). IRGC claims 200 US personnel killed. The conflict now directly involves Iran, the US, Israel, and multiple Gulf states with no de-escalation signals. Civilian casualties include 85+ children killed in Iranian school strikes.
whats_new: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei confirmed killed in US-Israeli bombardment of Tehran compound (South China Morning Post, Daily Sabah World)
IRGC announces seventh and eighth waves of 'Al-Wa'd Al-Sadeq 4' operation, signaling sustained retaliation campaign
UAE Defense Ministry reports 3 dead, 58 injured from Iranian strikes on UAE territory
Kuwaiti air defense intercepts hostile aerial targets in southern airspace
Hezbollah vows to continue resistance following Khamenei's death, escalating tensions on Israel's northern border
Iran fired missiles at Greek Cyprus hosting British troops, expanding geographic scope of conflict
Iran launches third wave of suicide drones targeting Dubai and Bahrain, hitting hotel and airport (Daily Mail)
Death toll from Iranian missile strike on Beit Shemesh, Israel rises to 6
confirmed_events: US and Israel launched coordinated airstrikes on over 20 Iranian provinces targeting government facilities and IRGC centers (Feb 28, 06:16 UTC)
Iranian ballistic missile barrages struck Israel (Haifa, Galilee, Beit Shemesh) on Feb 28
Iran struck 14 US military bases across Iraq, Syria, and Gulf region with missiles and drones
Iranian missile strikes hit US bases in Erbil (Iraq), Riyadh and Eastern Saudi Arabia, and Kuwait
IRGC Navy targeted US Navy support ship (MST) with missiles in Gulf region
US radar station in Qatar destroyed by Iranian drone strike
Iranian missile and suicide drone attacks struck Dubai (UAE) targeting Western civilians
US-Israeli airstrike destroyed Iranian Navy frigate at Khorramshahr base
Airstrike on school in southern Iran (Minab) killed at least 85 children
US and Israeli airstrikes killed senior Iranian military and intelligence officials (4+ senior figures)
IDF destroyed two Iranian fighter jets (F4, F5) on runway before takeoff
Oil tanker attacked off Oman coast; drones struck Duqm port, injuring 4 and evacuating 15 Indian crew
Red Crescent reports 747 injured and 201 killed across region (source: social media)
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims 2016 US military personnel killed in Iranian strikes on American bases — unverifiable, likely propaganda (IRGC statement)
Hezbollah Electronic Army claims Iranian rocket hit shelter in Beit Shemesh causing 'significant destruction' — limited corroboration
Iranian attacks on UAE results in 3 deaths and 58 injuries — corroboration limited to single TG source (War Monitors)
UK troops and civilians at risk from Iran strikes at base in Bahrain — specific casualty numbers unverified (BBC)
Iranian volleyball players strike (20 killed) — limited corroboration, single source cluster
Death of Khamenei's family members in strikes — extent unverified

people_travel: 131 Hong Kong residents stranded in Middle East due to flight cancellations (SCMP)
Dubai tourists forced to seek shelter in underground car parks during Iranian missile launches (Guardian)
Multiple international flights to/from Middle East suspended or rerouted
Pakistan PM cancels Russia trip due to regional tensions and security concerns
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz under threat — closure fears raise crude oil price concerns (The Hindu)
Duqm port (Oman) struck by drones, disrupting regional shipping
Oil tanker attacked off Oman coast — potential insurance/risk premium escalation
Gulf states actively intercepting missiles/drones — continued maritime threat
market_macro: Gas prices could rise by 25% due to Strait of Hormuz disruption (TASS)
UAE intercepts over 160 missiles since escalation began — significant defensive costs
GCC condemns Iranian attacks, indicating regional political/economic instability
Global oil supply chain at risk from conflict near critical chokepoint

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict trajectory indicates continued major escalation over the next 24-72 hours. Iran has lost its Supreme Leader and senior military leadership but has demonstrated continued offensive capability through waves 7-8 of 'Al-Wa'd Al-Sadeq 4' operations. IRGC will likely intensify strikes on US bases and Israel to demonstrate resolve under new leadership pressure. Hezbollah's vow to continue fighting suggests opening of a northern Israel front. The killing of Khamenei removes a restraint on Iranian escalation and may trigger unconventional responses. Expect sustained missile/drone exchanges across all fronts with continued civilian casualties.
escalation_risks: Khamenei's death may trigger Iranian use of unconventional weapons or proxies for attacks beyond current scope
Hezbollah opening full front against Israel could draw US forces deeper into Lebanon
Potential Iranian attacks on critical energy infrastructure (Gulf terminals, tankers) could close Strait of Hormuz
Russian/Chinese intervention or opportunistic moves in other theaters (Taiwan, Ukraine)
Iranian missiles targeting British troops in Cyprus could expand conflict to NATO members
Protests in Pakistan/Iraq at US consulates could create additional flashpoints
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation appears highly unlikely in the near term. The killing of Iran's Supreme Leader represents an irreversible political threshold that negates negotiation prospects. Iran has explicitly framed this as a war declaration. Any de-escalation would require either: (1) complete US-Israel military dominance achieving surrender, or (2) Iran achieving sufficient retaliation to claim victory. Regional diplomatic efforts (Jordan-Egypt-Oman coordination) remain underway but lack leverage. The most probable outcome is continued hostilities until one side's military capacity is substantially degraded.
